subject: GlowMug won't pair after firmware update
description: Customer updated the Northwind app and now the GlowMug shows a solid red light and never enters pairing mode when the base button is held.
resolution: Solid red after an update means the mug is mid firmware-flash. Instruct the customer to leave it on the charging coaster, untouched, for 10 minutes. If still red after 10 minutes, hold the base button for 15 seconds to force a restart, then retry pairing.

subject: GlowMug loses heat after ~40 minutes instead of 3 hours
description: Customer reports the mug stops heating well before the advertised 3-hour battery life, especially with the lid open.
resolution: Battery life is measured with the lid closed; leaving the lid open roughly triples power draw because the heating element compensates for heat loss. This is expected behavior, not a defect. Advise keeping the lid closed between sips.

subject: Refund not received after 10 business days
description: Customer returned a GlowMug three weeks ago, return was marked received, but no refund has appeared on their card statement.
resolution: Checked order history — refund was issued to a card that had since expired. Refunds to expired cards silently fail at the payment processor after ~5 days. Escalate to billing to reissue via store credit or updated card, and flag this failure mode is not currently visible to CS agents in the order dashboard.

subject: Exchange shipped wrong color
description: Customer exchanged a Slate Grey GlowMug for Arctic White but received another Slate Grey.
resolution: Confirmed warehouse mis-pick via order photos. Shipped correct Arctic White unit with expedited shipping at no cost and sent a prepaid label for the incorrect item — customer does not need to wait for the mispick to arrive before the correct one ships.

subject: App shows mug as 'offline' but mug is on and heating
description: Mug works fine physically (heats, beeps normally) but the Northwind app permanently shows 'Offline' status and won't let the customer change the temperature target.
resolution: This is caused by a stale Bluetooth pairing on the phone after the mug was previously paired to a different phone. Have the customer forget the 'GlowMug' device in their phone's Bluetooth settings entirely, then re-add it from within the Northwind app rather than the phone's Bluetooth menu.

subject: Remote work days not carrying over into new quarter
description: An employee's approved 4-day remote schedule reverted to the standard 3-day policy at the start of the new quarter.
resolution: Director-approved remote exceptions are reviewed and must be re-approved each quarter per the handbook remote work policy — this is intended, not a system bug. Employee should re-submit the exception request through their director for the new quarter.
